caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The frozen role mix is parent 5413 and its 48.44% residual contains the unlisted geoscientist, processor and field-crew roles that determine this estimate.
inputs.pi_dist: Neither source reports small-firm AI price realization; the value is an industry-structure estimate, not an observed pass-through rate.
inputs.pi_dist: TGS's data-library economics are deliberately not treated as representative of a $1–10M EBITDA contractor.
inputs.pi_moat: No source measures moat or customer switching for six-digit lower-middle-market firms.
inputs.pi_moat: Data rights and repeat-client concentration must be tested at each target.
inputs.t50_years: Technical evidence is not a firm-adoption census.
inputs.t50_years: The selection is positive because the paired current-adoption estimate is below the frozen 50% boundary.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: No code-specific current-adoption survey was found.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Use of established ML software by a geophysicist is not necessarily firm-wide effective adoption.
inputs.historical_analogs: This is a qualitative analogue, not evidence of a numeric adoption curve.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: No industry-specific owner-age evidence; validate seller age and succession at target level.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: A target may have a real succession need without an industry-wide documented shortage.
inputs.active_consolidators: The merger is between large public companies, not a small-firm add-on transaction.
inputs.active_consolidators: No recurring 541360 lower-middle-market acquisition program was confirmed.
inputs.buy_mult: Not an observed industry transaction multiple.
inputs.buy_mult: Asset ownership, backlog, client concentration and data rights can move a target outside this range.
inputs.exit_mult: Not an observed industry transaction multiple.
inputs.exit_mult: The uplift is contingent on target-specific backlog, data rights, field assets and customer concentration.
inputs.pricing_structure: The source does not disclose contract terms, margins or AI-driven repricing for small firms.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $274,931 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541360); class <5: 726 firms, avg revenue/firm $284,807 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $44,572 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 99 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,215,010 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $190,149 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 65 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,396,492 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $375,051 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 45 firms, avg revenue/firm $6,642,467 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,039,546 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 17 firms, avg revenue/firm $39,378,118 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $6,162,675 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 16 firms, avg revenue/firm $33,862,938 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $5,299,550 -> IN $1-10M band; n_band = 78

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: The frozen 4-digit mix is not representative of six-digit geophysics. The role judgments retain the prior bounded task view: ML materially affects processing/documentation, whereas field acquisition and geological accountability remain human; the residual is explicitly uncertain.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541360 ($1,461,125,114) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541360 ($1,992,974,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

S3's registered historical PDF URL did not render in the validator's direct fetch, but TGS's current official 2024 annual-report PDF exposes the same cited multi-client licensing and capitalization passages.
source_review.summary: Publishable with caveats. Exact-artifact mechanics, deterministic dataset equality, fresh finalization, memo rendering and arithmetic all pass. The reopened sources support the directional kill thesis: AI assists seismic processing and interpretation while expert geological input and physical acquisition remain, small firms compete through specialization, TGS's library economics are a deliberately bounded large-provider contrast, and the TGS-PGS merger is only one top-end event. Numeric adoption, capture, moat, ownership and multiple inputs remain plainly labeled low-confidence estimates; the disclosed uncertainties and the S3 URL-stability issue are non-fatal and do not reverse a factor or change the thesis.

sources.title: SEG: Introduction to Seismic Interpretation Using Deep Learning
sources.what_it_supports: SEG states that deep learning assists seismic-interpretation workflows and has value in common interpretation tasks; it supports technical feasibility, not a code-level adoption percentage.

sources.title: SEG: Seismic Attributes — from Interactive Interpretation to Machine Learning
sources.what_it_supports: SEG describes ML data reduction and classification in seismic interpretation and explains the continuing interpreter role in input, labels and geological interpretation.

sources.title: TGS 2024 Annual Report
sources.what_it_supports: TGS describes a multi-client library of geophysical/geological data licensed non-exclusively to oil companies, with acquisition and processing costs capitalized. It is evidence about the large data-library model, not small-firm economics or private transaction multiples.

sources.title: TGS and PGS Merger Completed
sources.what_it_supports: TGS reports that its merger with PGS formally completed on 1 July 2024; this documents one strategic consolidation event in energy geophysical data/services.

sources.title: First Research: Geophysical Surveying & Mapping Services Industry Profile
sources.what_it_supports: The profile identifies NAICS 541360 and describes the service mix as data collection, data sales and integrated geophysical services; it says small firms compete through geography or survey-type specialization while large firms gain scale.

title: Geophysical Surveying and Mapping Services
